summary: Backend regression + new-feature testing for Salt Check iteration 2. 43/44 pytest cases passed (97.7%). All previous 31 regression tests still green. New features verified: (1) PEPPER spice_level (mild/medium/extra_spicy) + nickname tuning - all spice levels return valid structured responses, crisis 'I want to die' overrides extra_spicy and contains safety language with no 'park' wording. (2) PEPPER -> daily_entries auto-sync confirmed: a POST to /api/pepper/checkin now creates a YYYY-MM-DD daily entry whose top_priorities mirror the first 3 salt_check items, next_sane_step is populated, money_action = ai.money_check, medication_note = ai.body_check, and water/food/hygiene default to False. Second same-day check-in UPDATES the existing entry (no duplicate; entry_id is stable) and preserves user-toggled survival checkboxes. (3) /api/pepper/transcribe accepts multipart wav upload, returns {"text": "..."} (with EMERGENT_LLM_KEY active - returned empty string for silent audio, which is expected), and returns 422 when no file is sent. (4) /api/send-test-push gracefully returns {"status": "failed", "reason": "..."} with the placeholder push key - no crash. (5) BUG FOUND: /api/register-push does NOT degrade gracefully with the placeholder EMERGENT_PUSH_KEY - it returns HTTP 500 with {"detail": "EMERGENT_PUSH_KEY missing or invalid"} instead of the contracted {"status": "skipped", "reason": "..."}.

critical.issue: When EMERGENT_PUSH_KEY is the placeholder, upstream returns 401 -> handler raises HTTPException(500) -> 'except HTTPException: raise' re-raises it -> client receives HTTP 500 {"detail": "EMERGENT_PUSH_KEY missing or invalid"}. Contract requires {"status": "skipped", "reason": "..."} with HTTP 200. Reference: /app/backend/server.py:772-786. Fix: remove the explicit 'except HTTPException: raise' OR convert the 401 branch to 'return {"status": "skipped", "reason": "EMERGENT_PUSH_KEY missing or invalid"}'.

minor.issue: Declared status_code=201, but returns 200 on the 'skipped' fallback path (FastAPI returns the model body but status_code stays 201 for raise_for_status path). Inconsistent. Reference: /app/backend/server.py:772.

Uses raw 'except Exception as e' wrapping the Whisper call - if the upstream key is missing this returns 500 with the raw error string. Consider returning 503 or a {"text": "", "error": "..."} graceful shape consistent with the push endpoints.

Daily-entry sync overwrites work_action/life_admin_action with None when the new salt_check has no matching keyword. If user has set these manually, they will be cleared on next PEPPER check-in (only water/food/hygiene checkboxes are preserved). Reference: /app/backend/server.py:419-437.
Carried over from iteration 1: ObjectId(invalid_string) raises bson.errors.InvalidId -> HTTP 500 instead of 404/400.

action_items: FIX /api/register-push so a placeholder EMERGENT_PUSH_KEY returns HTTP 200 {"status": "skipped", "reason": "..."} instead of HTTP 500. Either drop the 'except HTTPException: raise' block, or replace the inner 'raise HTTPException(500, ...)' with a direct return of the skipped payload.

Decide whether PEPPER auto-sync should preserve user-edited work_action/life_admin_action/money_action/medication_note (current behaviour overwrites them with whatever the new LLM call returns or None). Only water/food/hygiene checkboxes are preserved.
Consider aligning /api/pepper/transcribe error shape with the push endpoints (graceful JSON instead of HTTP 500) so the mobile client can render a friendly fallback.
Carry-over from iteration 1: wrap ObjectId() casts with InvalidId handling to return 404 on malformed ids.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py grew to 829 lines - past the 700-line guideline. Split into routers: pepper.py, push.py, daily.py, crud.py.

Push client uses module-level httpx.AsyncClient with no shutdown hook - clean shutdown will leave the connection open. Register an @app.on_event('shutdown') or use lifespan to await _push_client.aclose().
Duplicate logic between send_push() and the auto-sync block in pepper_checkin (both inspect salt_check items by keyword). Extract a small helper.
EMERGENT_PUSH_KEY default is the literal string 'placeholder' - that string then gets sent as the X-Push-Key header. Safer to leave it None and short-circuit before HTTP if missing.
Auto-sync overwrites top_priorities unconditionally with salt_check[:3]; if the user has hand-curated priorities they will be lost on next PEPPER call.
seed_data_creation: Test users prefixed TEST_spice_*, TEST_sync_*, TEST_push_user. PEPPER check-ins and daily entries are left in MongoDB (no DELETE endpoint). Projects/tasks/notes are cleaned up via teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL via /app/frontend/.env routes only /api/*. 13 new tests added in test_new_features.py. The single failure (test_register_push_does_not_crash) reflects a real bug, NOT a test issue - the endpoint returns 500 instead of 200+{status:skipped} when the push key is the placeholder. After main agent fixes the register-push graceful-failure path, re-run pytest /app/backend/tests/ - expect 44/44.
rca_of_issue: BUG: POST /api/register-push returns HTTP 500 with placeholder push key. Reproduction: curl -X POST $EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL/api/register-push -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"user_id":"u","platform":"android","device_token":"t"}' -> HTTP 500 {"detail":"EMERGENT_PUSH_KEY missing or invalid"}. Root cause: server.py:776-783 -- upstream returns 401 (placeholder key) -> 'raise HTTPException(500, ...)' inside try block -> immediately caught by 'except HTTPException: raise' which propagates it to the client, bypassing the generic 'except Exception' fallback that would have returned {"status":"skipped"}. Mitigation: change the 401 handler to 'return {"status":"skipped","reason":"EMERGENT_PUSH_KEY missing or invalid"}' OR delete the 'except HTTPException: raise' line so the generic handler catches it. The /api/send-test-push endpoint correctly handles this case because it wraps everything in 'except Exception as e' without a prior HTTPException re-raise.
